title: Local Input Security, Resource Limits, Privacy, and Diagnostics · Contract Workshop
summary: A museum puzzle team incrementally builds bounded local-data threat model and controls from original seeded fixtures while retaining earlier Python, graph, determinism, accessibility, testing, performance, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of bounded local-data threat model and controls while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify untrusted files, dimensions, strings, paths, package resources, environment state, logs, and denial-of-service consequences.
Use bounded data-only formats and reject pickle or executable object reconstruction for untrusted saves.
Validate dimensions, cell counts, edge counts, nesting, string length, numeric ranges, and total work before allocation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Commit to the mission model · Workshop Local Threat Model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop puzzle team handles case 6866 during a clean desktop release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to bound local inputs, reject executable serialization, constrain resources, and minimize diagnostics; all graph claims need an independent witness; then change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Identify untrusted files, dimensions, strings, paths, package resources, environment state, logs, and denial-of-service consequences.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-privacy-diagnostics-yjred6
# Competency: maze-privacy-diagnostics.
# Case 4725: a corrupted-state recovery drill.
# Operating constraint: all graph claims need an independent witness.
# Changed case: change the seed and retain replay identity.
def evidence_maze_privacy_diagnostics_yjred6(admitted_cells: int = 4096, maximum_cells: int = 16384, rejected_documents: int = 7, executable_formats: int = 0, retained_log_days: int = 7) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_yjred6 = "yjred6-6derjy-4725"
    assert evidence_variant_yjred6
    evidence_axes_yjred6 = (
        "gridyjred6",
        "topology4725",
        "search6derjy",
        "eventyjred64725",
        "access4725yjred6",
        "recovery6derjy4725",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_yjred6)) == 6
    if admitted_cells < 1 or maximum_cells < 1:
        return False, admitted_cells, "resource-shape"
    if admitted_cells > maximum_cells:
        return False, admitted_cells, "cell-budget"
    if rejected_documents < 1:
        return False, rejected_documents, "rejection-evidence"
    if executable_formats > 0 or retained_log_days > 30:
        return False, executable_formats, "local-trust"
    assert admitted_cells <= maximum_cells
    return True, admitted_cells, "bounded-local-data"
